schema.title: Response Logout Api Auth Logout Post
Response Change Password Api Auth Password Post

get.description:
Subset of the global Config the ripper reads on each disc insert to
decide whether to fire its scan/identify/rip pipeline. Cheap enough to
poll per-insert; avoids the WS-event invalidation dance for a single
boolean.

post.description:
Each ripper posts here every HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS with the
current CDROM_DRIVE_STATUS reading. The manual-trigger endpoint
reads `media_status` + `media_status_at` to refuse clicks made
against an empty / open tray, instead of letting identify land an
empty scan_result.

`last_seen_at` is bumped on every call so the drive's online state
is implicitly refreshed too — no separate liveness ping needed.

Phase 9 — per-job crash-recovery reset for the 'only ripper crashed'
case. Idempotent: re-running on an already-reset job is a no-op.
Returns the same shape as `rip-start` so the ripper's existing flow
continues unchanged.

get.description:
Phase 9 — boot-probe lookup. Returns the single RIPPING job assigned
to this drive, if any. 404 if the drive is unknown or no in-flight job
exists. Multiple matches (data-model violation) log + return the first.

schema.title: Response List Jobs Api Jobs Get

delete.description:
Hard-delete every job in a terminal status. Non-terminal jobs are
skipped and reported in `skipped_non_terminal` so the caller can
abandon-then-retry them.

`delete_raw=true` emits one `job.deleted` WS command per job to its
drive's ripper before the row is removed. Each rmtree is independent;
a failure on one ripper doesn't block deletes for the others.

Hard-delete a Job. Tracks, fingerprints, session_applications,
transcode_tasks, and events cascade via Postgres FK ondelete=CASCADE.

Refuses non-terminal jobs (CREATED / AWAITING_USER_ID / IDENTIFIED /
RIPPING) — caller must `POST /abandon` first if they want a job in
flight gone. This keeps the active-rip cancel logic in one place.

`delete_raw=true` also wipes `/raw/{job_id}/` on the ripper that
owns the drive (sent over WS — only the ripper has `/raw` mounted).
The DB delete proceeds regardless of whether the rmtree succeeds.

patch.description:
Edit user-controlled fields on a Job. Currently `poster_url_manual`
only — title/year are owned by the identify/resolve flow.

post.description:
Move a non-terminal job to `abandoned` and tell the ripper to clean up.

Two cases the ripper handles via the `job.abandoned` WS command:
  * AWAITING_USER_ID — the ripper is parked in `_await_resolution`;
    the waiter polls, sees the non-IDENTIFIED status, exits cleanly.
  * RIPPING — the ripper has an active scan/identify/rip pipeline. The
    WS handler cancels the asyncio task, which kills the makemkvcon
    subprocess so file handles release on `/raw/<id>/`.

`delete_raw` is plumbed in the WS payload because only the ripper has
`/raw` mounted; doing the rmtree here would silently no-op (and used to,
pre-fix). Even when there's no active task, the ripper still runs the
rmtree against any orphaned partial-rip directory.

Kick the ripper to run a job on a drive that already has a disc in
the tray. The ripper handles the WS command, scans the disc, and
threads `pending_session_id` through identify so the resulting Job's
metadata carries it. `rip-complete` then auto-applies that session.

schema.title: Response List Drives Api Drives Get
Response List Sessions Api Sessions Get
Response List Rip Presets Api Rip Presets Get
Response List Transcode Presets Api Transcode Presets Get
Response List Transcodes Api Transcodes Get

get.description:
In-memory zip with one entry per service that has matching lines.

Empty entries are omitted — a service whose log has zero lines for
this job does not show up at all.

NDJSON stream of every `/logs/*.log` line whose `job_id` matches.

Files are read in alphabetical order; within a file, lines are
yielded in append order. No cross-file resort — the consumer can
sort by `ts` if global ordering matters.

AbandonJobRequest.title: AbandonJobRequest
AbandonJobRequest.description:
POST /api/jobs/{id}/abandon body. `delete_raw=true` also wipes
`/raw/{job_id}/` so the drive can be reused without leftover partial
rips on disk. The DB row stays (status=abandoned) for audit.

BulkDeleteJobsResponse.title: BulkDeleteJobsResponse
BulkDeleteJobsResponse.description:
DELETE /api/jobs response. `deleted_ids` lists the jobs whose DB
rows were removed; `skipped_non_terminal` lists job IDs that were
refused because they were still in flight (caller should abandon
them first if they really want them gone).

ConfigView.title: ConfigView
ConfigView.description: `Config` row projected for the UI. `session_signing_key` is intentionally absent.

DiscFingerprintInput.title: DiscFingerprintInput
DiscFingerprintInput.description:
One (algo, value) pair on a scanned disc.

Canonical algo names: `crc64` (pydvdid DVD), `aacs` (Blu-ray AACS
Disc ID), `musicbrainz` (CD disc id), `matrix256` (ARM-native).
Free-form to permit new algos without a schema bump.

DriveMediaStatus.title: DriveMediaStatus
DriveMediaStatus.description:
What the drive thinks is in its tray. Reported by each ripper
on a heartbeat so the backend can fail manual-trigger requests
fast when the user clicks Start without loading a disc.

DriveUpdateRequest.title: DriveUpdateRequest
DriveUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH /api/drives/{id} body. Both fields optional + nullable.

`default_session_id=None` (explicit null) clears the field; omitting it
leaves it untouched. `extra="forbid"` keeps the API honest — UI typos
surface as 422 instead of being silently dropped.

HardwareCaps.title: HardwareCaps
HardwareCaps.description:
Self-reported transcoder capabilities. Phase 7 (CPU-only) ignores these;
Phase 7b consumes them for GPU dispatch + `gpus` row writes.

JobCompleteRequest.title: JobCompleteRequest
JobCompleteRequest.description:
POST /api/ripper/jobs/{job_id}/rip-complete body.

Empty for now; backend computes the final job status from the track
outcomes. Reserved for future flags (e.g. user-initiated abort).

JobUpdateRequest.title: JobUpdateRequest
JobUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH /api/jobs/{id} body. Currently only the manual poster override
is editable — title/year live behind the identify/resolve flow.

ManualTriggerRequest.title: ManualTriggerRequest
ManualTriggerRequest.description:
POST /api/jobs/manual — kick off a rip on a drive that already has a
disc in the tray. The ripper picks it up via WS command and runs the
normal scan→identify→rip flow; the optional `session_id` is stamped on
the resulting Job's metadata so `rip-complete` auto-applies it.

RegisterTranscoderResponse.title: RegisterTranscoderResponse
RegisterTranscoderResponse.description:
Bootstrap bundle the transcoder needs before it can claim + run.

`raw_input_path` is the absolute path the transcoder should read from
(`/raw/<job>/title_tNN.mkv` for video, `/raw/<job>/track_NN.wav` for
audio, `/raw/<job>/dump.iso` for data). `media_root` plus the task's
`output_path` is where the final file lands.

RipperConfigView.title: RipperConfigView
RipperConfigView.description:
Subset of `Config` the ripper reads to gate its automatic behaviour.

Polled on disc-insert before the pipeline kicks off; small enough to
keep cheap. Does not include any UI-only fields.

RipperHeartbeatRequest.title: RipperHeartbeatRequest
RipperHeartbeatRequest.description:
POST /api/ripper/heartbeat body. Each ripper sends one of these
every HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_SECONDS so the backend can refuse manual-
trigger requests against drives whose tray is open / empty without
waiting for a doomed identify.

Named distinctly from the transcoder HeartbeatRequest because the
two services post completely different payloads to different paths.

TrackUpdateRequest.title: TrackUpdateRequest
TrackUpdateRequest.description:
PATCH /api/ripper/tracks/{track_id} body.

Only fields that are non-None are written. Backend validates the
state-machine transition implied by `status`.
